draft_v1: {"schema_v":1,"recipe":{"title":"BEEF AND BARLEY STEW","yield_phrase":"SERVES 4 TO 6"},"steps":[{"instruction":"1. Toss the short ribs in a large bowl with salt and pepper to coat. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven over high heat until smoking. Add the beef and cook, without moving it, until well browned on first side, about 5 minutes. Stir the beef and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until browned all over, about 10 minutes total; reduce the heat if the bottom of the pot begins to scorch. Return the meat to the bowl and set aside.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 pounds boneless beef short ribs, cut into 1-inch chunks"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 tablespoons canola oil"}]},{"instruction":"2. Return the pot to medium-high heat and add the carrots, celery, and onion. Cook, stirring frequently, until the vegetables begin to brown, about 4 minutes. Add the Marmite, soy sauce, garlic, and tomato paste and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 30 seconds.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 medium carrots, peeled, split in half lengthwise, and sliced into 1/2-inch pieces (about 1 cup)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 medium stalks celery, split in half lengthwise, and sliced into 1/2-inch pieces (about 1 cup)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 large onion, finely diced (about 11/2 cups)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1/2 teaspoon Marmite"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 teaspoon soy sauce"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 medium cloves garlic, minced or grated on a Microplane (about 2 teaspoons)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 tablespoon tomato paste"}]},{"instruction":"3. Add the stock and scrape up the browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon. Add the tomatoes, barley, and bay leaves, then return the beef to the pot, increase the heat to high, and bring to a boil. Reduce to the lowest possible heat and cover the pot, leaving the lid slightly ajar. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the beef is completely tender and the barley is cooked through, about 2 hours.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 cups homemade or low-sodium canned chicken stock"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"One 141/2-ounce can whole tomatoes, drained and roughly chopped"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 cup pearl barley"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 bay leaves"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 cups loosely packed roughly torn kale leaves"}]}]}
